skillName: Use formal methods for addition of decimals
misconceptionSummary: Students align the decimal points but then fail to pad, leaving gaps in the column structure.

lines.text: Aligned points but left a gap in tenths column

steps.narration: The decimal points are aligned but three point six has no digits in the hundredths or thousandths columns. Without padding, the student's eye skips across and pairs the wrong digits.

lines.text: Now both have three decimal places

steps.narration: Pad three point six with two trailing zeros to make three point six zero zero. Both numbers now have three decimal places — every column has a digit and there are no gaps to misread.
Zero plus seven is seven, zero plus five is five, six plus four is ten — write zero carry one, three plus one plus one is five. Answer: five point zero five seven.

visuals.label: Align points → pad both to same d.p. → add columns

steps.narration: Five point zero five seven. Padding to equal decimal places eliminates any ambiguity about which digits belong in the same column.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: aligning decimal points but not padding — leaving gaps that cause wrong column pairings. Always pad both numbers to the same number of decimal places before adding.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Aligning points is step one; padding is step two. Without padding, gaps in the layout cause digits to pair with the wrong columns.
